reasoning: The Lean statement directly captures "not comparable" as "neither topology is contained in the other": `¬ (∀ U, Rl.IsOpen U → K_topology.IsOpen U)` says Rl ⊄ K_topology (some Rl-open set isn't K-open), and `¬ (∀ U, K_topology.IsOpen U → Rl.IsOpen U)` says K_topology ⊄ Rl. Their conjunction is exactly the standard definition of two topologies being incomparable. The supporting definitions match Munkres: the lower limit topology is generated by {[a,b) : a<b} (Set.Ico in Mathlib); the K-topology is generated by {(a,b)} ∪ {(a,b)\K}; and K = {1/(n+1) : n ∈ ℕ} = {1, 1/2, 1/3, …} coincides with Munkres' K = {1/n : n ∈ ℤ₊}. The a<b requirement in the basis is harmless since the empty set is open in any topology. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure correctly expresses incomparability.
